articles.title: Punjab reduces power cut duration to 4 hours amid heatwave - The Tribune
articles.search_term: heatwave (Amritsar OR Barnala OR Bathinda OR Faridkot OR "Fatehgarh Sahib" OR Fazilka OR Ferozepur OR Gurdaspur OR Hoshiarpur OR Jalandhar OR Kapurthala OR Ludhiana OR Mansa OR Moga OR Muktsar OR "Nawanshahr (Shahid Bhagat Singh Nagar)" OR Pathankot OR Patiala OR Rupnagar OR "Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar (Mohali)" OR Sangrur OR "Tarn Taran") Punjab
articles.full_text:

Take your experience further with Premium access. Thought-provoking Opinions, Expert Analysis, In-depth Insights and other Member Only BenefitsA day after a lot of hullabaloo over scheduled power cuts across Punjab — ranging from six to over eight hours for scheduled maintenance works in peak heat wave days — and reported long power snags, the Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL) has issued new directions “limiting such works” and ensuring not more than four hours of power cuts in a day.
According to new orders issued by the PSPCL director (generation), new protocol has to be followed for planned outages for maintenance and new works.
“It has been observed that the planned outages for maintenance and new works recently submitted by the zonal offices are proposed for duration of six to eight hours. However, in wake of the sudden increase in temperature and in order to provide relief to the public, the management has decided that no new work shall exceed more than four hours per day,” read the new order, a copy of which is with The Tribune.
Further, the orders state that all work during shutdown period must be supervised by an AE ranked officer. “To prevent time wastage, all required material should be at the work site one day prior to the scheduled work. Officers of senior XEN shall conduct random/occasional site visits to insect quality and progress of the work,” it reads further.
The new order also states that every outage, with proper entry of all fields, must be mapped on the distribution portal. “These instructions are effective immediately to ensure minimal grievance to the general public during the ongoing heatwave and are applicable to the schedule already submitted for planned outages of this week,” concludes the letter.
The new order came after the earlier notice, when the PSPCL had issued a circular scheduling long-duration outages of six to eight hours till April 26 in major cities, including Ludhiana, Jalandhar, Amritsar, Bathinda and Patiala. However, even before work began in some areas, residents complained of frequent and prolonged outages during afternoon hours and at night.
This had led to protests against the government at many places and the opposition parties had also questioned the move of disruption in power supply during peak summer days.
Data from the Northern Regional Load Despatch Centre (NRLDC) showed that peak demand met on April 20 was 9,360 MW, with supply at 1,798 lakh units. On Wednesday, demand rose to 10,353 MW, compared to 9,581 MW last year, leading to some power supply shortage. However, PSPCL claims that they have ample power available.
